title: Traffic-Pocket And Rejoin-Position Game Features
summary: F1 strategy sources repeatedly note that the success of an undercut depends on rejoining in clean air, not just tyre age.
rationale: F1 strategy sources repeatedly note that the success of an undercut depends on rejoining in clean air, not just tyre age. That suggests adding local opponent context: whether pitting now likely drops the driver into traffic, or whether a rival ahead is vulnerable to a clean-air undercut.
implementation_hint: Within each Year-Race-LapNumber snapshot, build field-relative features: gaps in current position order, density of cars within a small lap-time/position window, estimated pit-window pocket size, cars likely to be jumped after a stop, and a crude clean-air score using nearby rivals’ tyre age/pace. Use only same-lap information; do not leak future pit reactions.
expected_effect: Should improve ranking among medium-probability cases where two drivers have similar tyre wear but only one has a strategically usable pit window.
risk: Without exact time gaps, proxy traffic features may be noisy; benefit depends on whether position/lap-time surrogates are strong enough to approximate rejoin traffic.
